TIER_CLASSIFICATION: 3 — heavy (20+ files touched across 6 modules, retrospective). Should have been sub-dispatched but completed in a single Opus session because each fix exposed the next and the cumulative graph wasn't visible up-front. Token budget held just under 120K.

PHASE_4_SCAFFOLD_BUGS_FIXED_THIS_SESSION:
  1. packages/storage used `./xxx.js` extensions in imports → broke Next webpack resolution. Fix: removed `.js` extensions to match db/jobs/shared monorepo style.
  2. apps/web/next.config.ts transpilePackages missing @yelli/storage. Fix: added.
  3. Middleware imported full @/server/auth → pulled bcrypt + Prisma into Edge bundle. Fix: split into auth.config.ts (edge-safe shell) + auth.ts (full server). Canonical Auth.js v5 pattern.
  4. bcrypt 5.1.1 had 6 HIGH CVEs (tar transitive via @mapbox/node-pre-gyp) + native-binding install fragility. Fix: drop-in swap to bcryptjs ^2.4.3 (pure JS, same API). Zero HIGH CVEs after.
  5. packages/db/prisma/seed.ts used named `import { hash } from 'bcrypt'` but bcryptjs has no named hash export. Fix: default import + bcrypt.hash(...). Was silent — seed failed at module-load with cryptic ESM error.
  6. apps/web/src/env.ts ran serverSchema.safeParse() at module-load even in browser → client components importing env (e.g. turnstile-widget) crashed. Fix: typeof window === "undefined" guard.
  7. .env.dev had SMTP_FROM_NAME=Yelli Dev (unquoted space → source fails) + SMTP_FROM=dev@localhost (Zod .email() rejects, no TLD) + PGADMIN_EMAIL=dev-admin@yelli.local (RFC 2606 reserved). Fix: quote the value, use dev@yelli.test + dev-admin@yelli.com.
  8. apps/web/src/env.ts schema required NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL but .env.dev didn't have it. Fix: appended to .env.dev.
  9. apps/web/src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx used useSearchParams() without Suspense — Next 15 App Router static prerender requires Suspense boundary. Fix: extracted LoginForm, wrapped with Suspense.
 10. apps/web/src/components/speed-dial/speed-dial-grid.tsx empty-state link `/app/settings/departments/new` (route never built) → 404. Fix: redirected to `/admin/departments` where the actual UI lives.
 11. Build-time env loading: Prisma seed and Next build couldn't see .env.dev because Next only auto-loads `.env`/`.env.local`/`.env.development`. Fix: (a) `tsx --env-file-if-exists=../../.env.dev` for seed; (b) symlink `apps/web/.env.local` → `.env.dev` for Next.

ROOT_CAUSE_OF_BUILD_FAILURE_THAT_LOOKED_LIKE_LIVEKIT:
  The error path `.next/browser/default-stylesheet.css` framed the issue as a LiveKit CSS problem for ~90 minutes. After 7 dead-end CSS-loading approaches (transpilePackages variations, exports-map wildcards, globals.css @import, relative file path, static <link>), grep on the actual chunk revealed:
    p = e.readFileSync(f.resolve(__dirname, "../../browser/default-stylesheet.css"), ...)
  Source: jsdom (transitive via isomorphic-dompurify). jsdom does fs.readFileSync at module load. Webpack bundled jsdom, flattened directory structure → relative path broke during page-data collection. Fix: serverExternalPackages: ["isomorphic-dompurify", "jsdom"]. The LiveKit dynamic-loader pattern + static-CSS approach were preserved as defense-in-depth though they weren't the root cause.
